ASBESTOS JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Jan-09-2012 Continued To Jan-10-12 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 602
MINI MINUTES: Judge Suzanne Bolanos, Clerk Karissa Shaw, Not Reported. (602)

APPEARANCES:
Chris Hersom of Brayton Purcell representing the plaintiffs.
Constance Morrison of Jackson Jenkins Renstrom LLP representing HB Smith.

At 2:30 p.m., this matter comes on calendar for a status/pre-trial conference. Court and counsel confer on settlement status in this case. Jury harshipping will be conducted on 1/10/12 at 1:30 p.m. and 1/11/12 at 9:30 a.m. Motions in limine will be heard on 1/11/12 at 2:30 p.m. If this matter settles, then a status conference will be held 1/11/12 at 9:30 a.m.

At 2:37 p.m., matter is adjourned.

ASBESTOS JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Jan-06-2012 Continued To Jan-09-12 At 02:30 P.M. In Dept 602
MINI MINUTES: Judge Suzanne Bolanos, Clerk Karissa Shaw, Not Reported. (602)

APPEARANCES:
Chris Hersom of Brayton Purcell representing the plaintiffs.
Constance F. Morrison of Jackson Jenkins Renstrom LLP representing HB Smith (Gisler).
Ian Dillon of Walsworth Franklin Bevins & McCall LLP representing JM Manufacturing (Fagg).
Jeremy D. Huie of Bassi Edlin Huie & Blum LLP representing JT Thorpe & Son (Fagg) and Marc Paul (Buford).

10:00 a.m., this matter comes on calendar for a status conference. Walsh vs. Kaiser (274824) settled on 1/5/12. Gisler and Fagg (275481) are the next to be tried and Buford (274176) is trailing.

All motions in limine that need to be heard before trial have already been filed with the court. Deposition designations are also discussed.

***Monday (1/9/12) at 2:00 p.m. is the final settlement deadline.***

A status conference will be held 1/9/12 at 2:30 p.m. Hardships will be conducted Tuesday (1/10/12) at 9:30 a.m. Copies of the juror questionnaires are due in Dept. 602 by 1/9/12 at 2:30 p.m. Voir Dire will be conducted on 1/12/12 and possibly 1/13/12 as well as hearing motions in limine that need be ruled upon before trial. The trial is estimated to last 4-5 weeks.

Another status conference in all three (3) cases is set for 1/11/12 at 9:30 a.m.

Buford will be trailing Fagg and Gisler. Therefore, Buford counsel must be ready for trial if the other two (2) cases settle beforehand or a verdict is reached at trial. The 1/9/12 settlement deadline and status conference also apply to Buford counsel.

At 10:30 a.m., this matter is adjourned until Monday, 1/9/12, at 2:30 p.m. for a status/pre-trial conference.

APPEARANCES:
James Nevin of Brayton Purcell representing the plaintiffs.

This matter comes on calendar for a status conference. This case will continue to trail the Walsh matter (274824) for trial. Case is continued for another status conference to January 11, 2012 at 9:30 a.m.

Off Calendar Dec-13-2011 Continued To Dec-14-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 602
MINI MINUTES: Judge Suzanne Bolanos, Clerk Karissa Shaw, Not Reported. (602)

***Brayton Group 581***

APPEARANCES:
James Nevin and Chris Hersom of Brayton Purcell representing the plaintiffs.
Brendan Tuohy of K&L Gates representing Crane Co. (List case)
Jason Molinelli of Archer Norris representing Moore Bros.
Constance Morrison of Jackson Jenkins Renstrom representing HB Smith.

Defendant Crane Co. has resolved out of all of the cases. Brayton Group 578 (LC #274139, Teale case - wrongful death) will go to trial first.

At 3:14 p.m., court and counsel confer on the juror questionnaire to be used for hardship as well as a proposed joint statement of the case. There are no motions in limine that need to be heard before voir dire. Juror hardships will be conducted 12/14/11, voir dire on 12/19/11 and 12/20/11, motions in limine will be heard on 1/3/12 and trial will commence on 1/4/12.

The court recess from 3:24 p.m. to 3:37 p.m. in order for counsel to finalize the juror questionnaire.

Court and counsel confer on the questionnaire. Copies of the finalized version must be provided to the court by tomorrow (12/14/11) at 9:00 a.m. in Department 602. Counsel have also finalized a generic statement of the case. Counsel estimate the trial will last six weeks including deliberations. There will be 20 days worth of evidence. All counsel are ordered present for jury hardshipping. Plaintiffs' counsel is allotted one hour for voir dire and defense counsel will confer on possibly conducting a joint voir dire with their time.

Court and counsel confer on deposition designation and counter-designations. The court orders a briefing schedule on it. Counsel in living asbestos cases should also abide by all deadlines set out in today's proceedings.

At 3:57 p.m., this matter is adjourned.
